description: A shared process is 'thunesi' when its contributions happen at different times, so participants need not be present together. A written discussion may be asynchronous even when replies follow an order and arrive before a deadline. 'wiresu' (postpone) moves a planned event later; asynchrony is the timing pattern of the process itself.
articulatory_notes: Rest the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th', let air pass along it, and finish the first syllable with high rounded back 'u'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and move into stressed true-mid front unrounded 'e'. Raise the tongue blade towards the alveolar ridge for unvoiced 's', direct air through the narrow channel, and finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.
usage_notes: The adjective applies to a contribution as well as the shared process that receives it. In noun use, the form is asynchrony. A deadline and an expected reply order remain separate features of the arrangement.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A garden log can pass observations from one shift to the next without gathering everyone around one clock.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Time to read and compose can be part of participation rather than evidence that someone missed the exchange.

semantic_domains.activity: contributions joining one process at different times
semantic_domains.communication: an exchange designed for replies without simultaneous presence
